metadata.description: Primitive ID to Python mapping (runtime version)

1083.name: Boolean Constant
1083.note: 0 inputs, 1 Boolean output. Always primIdx 377-381. Constant primitive - actual value (True/False) determined by VI data, not primResID.

1104.name: Greater?
1104.note: Same as 1102 but for arrays/clusters. Same semantics.

1142.name: Type Conversion
1142.note: 1in/1out float->int conversion. Exact function unconfirmed. Python int() as approximation.

1056.name: Split 1D Array
1056.note: Connector pane: idx=2 is index (numeric), idx=3 is array. Confirmed from pco.camera_FloatingMean_Example.vi instances.

1145.name: Coerce To Type
1145.note: 1in/1out type conversion. Python is dynamically typed so this is a passthrough.

1516.name: Select
1516.note: Select function: result = t_value if selector else f_value. primResID=1516 primIndex=136. All 334 class='prim' instances are Select; real Array Subset uses class='subset' (node_type resolution).

1608.name: Flatten To String
1608.note: Flatten To String serializes data. In Python, str() is a rough equivalent.

1999.name: Not A Path
1999.note: Constant that returns the <Not A Path> value. In Python, None represents an invalid/empty path.

python_code.substring: in_0[in_1:] if in_3 is None else in_0[in_1:in_1 + in_3]

1167.name: Boolean To (0,1)
1167.note: Polymorphic: Boolean -> I16 (1 or 0). Array of Boolean -> Array of I16. primIndex=69.

1053.name: Divide
1053.note: primIndex=106. Polymorphic. Per docs: int/int produces Float64 (matches our 22+ observed instances of NumInt32/NumInt32 -> NumFloat64). Python `/` operator has same semantics.

1111.name: Less Or Equal?
1111.note: primIndex=114. Polymorphic numeric comparison. 7 observed signatures across all integer/float type pairs.

8056.name: File Dialog
8056.note: Interactive UI primitive. Original LabVIEW intent was to ask the user, so we map to Python's input() — runs in CLI, easy to swap with hardcoded Path() or tkinter.filedialog. Configuration Booleans (idx=9, 10) for file/folder/new-or-existing modes are not implemented.

1113.name: Equal To 0?
1113.note: Polymorphic variant of 1115. Output is Boolean for scalar, Boolean array for array input.

python_code.path: Path(*in_0) if not in_1 else Path(*in_0)

python_code.filenames: [f.name for f in Path(in_11).iterdir() if f.is_file()]

python_code.result_string: in_1.replace(in_2, in_3) if in_4 else in_1.replace(in_2, in_3, 1)

python_code.substring_before_match: in_4[:in_4.find(in_5, in_3)] if in_4.find(in_5, in_3) >= 0 else in_4
python_code.match_rest_of_string: in_4[in_4.find(in_5, in_3):] if in_4.find(in_5, in_3) >= 0 else ''

1116.name: Call Chain
1116.note: 1in/1out numeric→boolean. In comparison range but exact function unconfirmed. Passthrough for now.

1170.name: Split Number
1170.note: 1in/2out array→array+array. Could be Split Number or type-specific split. Unconfirmed.

1120.name: Sort 1D Array
1120.note: primIndex=206. In OpenG Sort wrappers, sorts ascending; the separately-located 1900 primitive applies Reverse 1D Array to flip to descending. Semantics confirmed: Frame 0 (ascending) passes through 1120 output; Frame 1 (descending) applies 1900 Reverse after 1120 Sort.

1900.name: Reverse 1D Array
1900.note: Polymorphic variant of 1902 (Reverse 1D Array). primIndex=207. Used in OpenG Sort wrappers to flip ascending sort to descending (case frame labeled "descending").

1128.name: Not A Number/Path/Refnum?
1128.note: Polymorphic variant of 1112 for Refnum type.

9102.name: Close Reference
9102.note: Close Reference variant with path output. Same as 9104 but returns path.

9105.name: Call By Reference
9105.note: Calls a VI by reference. In Python, equivalent to calling the loaded module's function.

8003.name: Variant To Data
8003.note: primResID 8003 is file I/O range but this instance has variant terminals. Variant To Data confirmed by terminal signature and PDF.
